item.title: Buddhist Education: Paccaya Foundation to Launch Dharma-Inspired School for Children in Bangkok
item.description: Craig Lewis 4 February 2026 Paccaya Foundation is marking its 10th anniversary with the August 2026 launch of The Jataka School, an early-years and primary school in Bangkok.

item.title: Buddhist Monks on 100th Day of Walk for Peace Across the US Reach Richmond, Virginia
item.description: Justin Whitaker 5 February 2026 Local and state leaders welcomed the monks and emphasized peace as a daily practice

item.title: Shugsep Nunnery in India to Hold its First-everKhenmoEnthronement Ceremony for 19 Buddhist Nuns
item.description: Shugsep Nunnery in India to Hold its First-ever Khenmo Enthronement Ceremony for 19 Buddhist Nuns Craig Lewis 6 February 2026 The Tibetan Nuns Project has announced that Shugsep Nunnery will hold its first-ever khenmo enthronement ceremony for 19 nuns on 18 February.

item.title: Dalai Lama’s Office Issues Statement Refuting Speculation About Ties to Jeffrey Epstein
item.description: Craig Lewis 9 February 2026 The Office of His Holiness the Dalai Lama issued a statement firmly rejecting attempts to link His Holiness to disgraced American financier Jeffrey Epstein.

item.title: Pasadena Buddhist Temple Commissions Fire-Displaced Artist to Lead Community Healing Mural
item.description: Justin Whitaker 10 February 2026 Pasadena Buddhist Temple, a member of the Buddhist Churches of America, has launched a participatory mural for those affected by last year’s massive Eaton Fire in southern California

item.title: Buddhist Studies: Buddha-Dharma Centre of Hong Kong Invites Applicants for Master’s Degree and Diploma Programs
item.description: Craig Lewis 11 February 2026 The Buddha-Dharma Centre of Hong Kong is inviting applications for its Master of Arts in Buddhist Studies and Diploma in Buddhist Studies programs.

item.title: Buddhist Monks Complete Historic Walk for Peace to Washington, DC
item.description: Sensei Alex Kakuyo 12 February 2026 The monks’ 108-day journey ends with interfaith ceremonies at the Washington National Cathedral and a planned gathering at the Lincoln Memorial

item.title: Berkeley Zen Center to Install First Female Abbot in Historic Leadership Transition
item.description: Craig Lewis 13 February 2026 In a significant milestone for the Berkeley Zen Center, Shinchi Linda Galijan will be installed as its first female abbot on 1 March.

item.title: Vajrakilaya Dubchen Held at Buddha Dordenma Kuensel Phodrang from 15–21 February
item.description: Raymond Lam 16 February 2026 Held to celebrate His Majesty the Fifth King’s forty-sixth birthday, the Vajrakilaya Great Accomplishment Ritual sweeps away all obstacles

item.title: Buddhist Monk’s Two-Decade Support Credited in South Korea’s Olympic Snowboarding Success
item.description: Justin Whitaker 20 February 2026 Ven. Hosan of Bongseonsa Temple founded the Dharma Snowboard Competition to help young athletes cover training costs

item.title: Ngari Rinpoche, Youngest Brother of the Dalai Lama, Dies Aged 80
item.description: Craig Lewis 23 February 2026 Tendzin Choegyal, the 16th Ngari Rinpoche and youngest brother of His Holiness the 14th Dalai Lama, died on 17 February in Dharamsala, India.

item.title: Buddhist Monks’ Walk for Peace in India Reaches Hyderabad
item.description: Justin Whitaker 24 February 2026 The third Dhamma Padayatra emphasizes interfaith harmony and renewed engagement with Buddhist teachings in India
